1‚Üí---
     2‚Üíname: frontend-impl
     3‚Üídescription: |
     4‚Üí  Activate this skill when:
     5‚Üí  - Building DashboardOverview.tsx
     6‚Üí  - Writing React/TypeScript code
     7‚Üí  - Integrating with Living Apps API
     8‚Üíallowed-tools:
     9‚Üí  - Read
    10‚Üí  - Write
    11‚Üí  - Edit
    12‚Üí  - Bash
    13‚Üí  - Glob
    14‚Üí  - Grep
    15‚Üí---
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí# Frontend Implementation Skill
    18‚Üí
    19‚ÜíBuild a **production-ready, domain-specific dashboard** as the app's primary workspace.
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí---
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí## Step 1: Analyze and Decide (MANDATORY ‚Äî before any code)
    24‚Üí
    25‚ÜíRead `.scaffold_context` and `app_metadata.json`. Then write 1-2 sentences describing:
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí1. **What is the best UI paradigm for the user's core workflow?**
    28‚Üí2. **Why is this the most natural way to interact with THIS data?**
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíUse this table to guide your choice:
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí| Data Nature | Best UI Paradigm |
    33‚Üí|-------------|-----------------|
    34‚Üí| Time-based / scheduled entries | Calendar, week planner, timeline |
    35‚Üí| Status-based / workflow stages | Kanban board, progress pipeline |
    36‚Üí| Quantitative / goal-tracking | Progress rings, gauges, trend charts |
    37‚Üí| Hierarchical / categorized | Grouped sections, nested views |
    38‚Üí| Sequential / step-by-step | Stepper, checklist, flow view |
    39‚Üí| Relational / many linked items | Master-detail, linked cards |
    40‚Üí
    41‚ÜíThen implement immediately. No design_brief.md, no task lists, no planning documents.
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí---
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí## Step 2: Edit Layout.tsx (title only)
    46‚Üí
    47‚ÜíUse Edit to change `APP_TITLE` and `APP_SUBTITLE`. Nothing else.
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí## Step 3: Build DashboardOverview.tsx
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí**Mandatory sequence:**
    52‚Üí1. **Read** `src/pages/DashboardOverview.tsx` using the Read tool
    53‚Üí2. **Write** `src/pages/DashboardOverview.tsx` ONCE with the complete content
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí**NEVER use Bash (cat/echo/heredoc) for file operations.** If Read or Write fails, retry with the same tool.
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí## Step 4: Deploy
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí```bash
    60‚Üínpm run build
    61‚Üí```
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíThen call `mcp__deploy_tools__deploy_to_github`
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí---
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí## What Is Pre-Generated (DO NOT touch!)
    68‚Üí
    69‚ÜíCRUD sub-pages, dialogs, routing, sidebar, shared components, and the design system are pre-generated.
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí**DO NOT touch:** index.css, CRUD pages, dialogs, App.tsx, PageShell.tsx, StatCard.tsx, ConfirmDialog.tsx, ChatWidget.tsx, useDashboardData.ts, enriched.ts, enrich.ts, formatters.ts, ai.ts, chat-context.ts.
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí**EDITABLE:** `src/config/ai-features.ts` ‚Äî toggle `AI_PHOTO_SCAN['EntityName'] = true` to enable the "Foto scannen" button in that entity's create/edit dialog. Useful for entities where users may photograph documents, receipts, or business cards to auto-fill form fields.
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí`index.css` contains the shared design system (Plus Jakarta Sans, indigo palette, dark sidebar). All semantic tokens (`bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-sidebar`, etc.) are ready to use. Do NOT edit index.css ‚Äî use existing tokens in your components.
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí**Already available in DashboardOverview.tsx:**
    78‚Üí- `useDashboardData()` ‚Äî all entities loaded, lookup maps built, loading/error handled
    79‚Üí- `enrichX()` ‚Äî applookup fields resolved to display name strings
    80‚Üí- `formatDate()`, `formatCurrency()` ‚Äî locale-aware formatting
    81‚Üí- Loading skeleton and error state with retry
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí**AI utilities available in `src/lib/ai.ts`:**
    84‚Üí- `chatCompletion()` ‚Äî core LLM call
    85‚Üí- `classify()` ‚Äî auto-categorize text
    86‚Üí- `extract()` ‚Äî structured data from text
    87‚Üí- `summarize()` ‚Äî condense text
    88‚Üí- `translate()` ‚Äî translate text
    89‚Üí- `analyzeImage()`, `extractFromPhoto()` ‚Äî image analysis
    90‚Üí- `analyzeDocument()` ‚Äî PDF/document analysis
    91‚Üí- `fileToDataUri()` ‚Äî encode File for AI calls
    92‚Üí- `safeJsonCompletion()`, `withRetry()` ‚Äî error handling
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí---
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí## Dashboard = Primary Workspace, NOT Info Page
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí**The #1 mistake is building the dashboard as a passive info screen** (KPI cards + chart + recent activity). Users want to WORK with their data, not just look at it.
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí### The Core Interactive Component
   101‚Üí
   102‚ÜíEvery dashboard needs ONE interactive component ‚Äî the **reason users open the app**. This component:
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí- Takes up significant screen space (hero, not sidebar widget)
   105‚Üí- Supports create, edit, delete directly (click empty slot ‚Üí create dialog, click entry ‚Üí edit)
   106‚Üí- Shows data in its most natural form (the paradigm you chose in Step 1)
   107‚Üí- Provides immediate visual feedback
   108‚Üí
   109‚ÜíThe pre-generated CRUD list pages are a fallback. Users should do 90% of their work without leaving the dashboard.
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí**ALWAYS reuse pre-generated dialogs** ‚Äî When the dashboard needs create/edit forms, import `{Entity}Dialog` from `@/components/dialogs/{Entity}Dialog`. Never build custom dialog forms from scratch ‚Äî the pre-generated ones already have all field types, photo scan, validation, and applookup selects.
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí### Anti-Slop Checklist (if ANY true, redesign!)
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí- Dashboard is a passive info page ‚Äî only KPI cards and charts
   116‚Üí- No domain-specific UI ‚Äî uses generic list/table for core data
   117‚Üí- All KPI cards look identical
   118‚Üí- Layout is a boring 2x2 or 3x3 grid
   119‚Üí- No clear hero element
   120‚Üí- Colors are generic blue/green/red (use the pre-configured palette tokens instead)
   121‚Üí- Dashboard could be for ANY app
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí---
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí## Design Principles
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí### Theme
   128‚Üí
   129‚ÜíFont (Plus Jakarta Sans) and color palette (indigo accent, warm off-white base, dark sidebar) are pre-configured in `index.css`. Use existing semantic tokens ‚Äî do NOT add custom CSS variables unless the dashboard requires truly app-specific values (e.g. `--calendar-slot-height`).
   130‚Üí
   131‚ÜíCreate typography hierarchy through weight differences (font-300 vs font-700) and size jumps (text-2xl vs text-sm).
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí### Layout: Visual Interest Required
   134‚Üí
   135‚ÜíEvery layout needs variation ‚Äî size, weight, spacing, format, typography. If everything is the same size in identical cards, it's AI slop.
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üí**Mobile:** Vertical flow, thumb-friendly, hero dominates first viewport.
   138‚Üí**Desktop:** Use horizontal space, multi-column where appropriate, hover reveals.
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üí---
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí## Pre-Generated Component APIs (exact props ‚Äî do NOT Read to check, do NOT guess)
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí**`{Entity}Dialog`** ‚Äî always this exact interface:
   145‚Üí```tsx
   146‚Üí<KurseDialog
   147‚Üí  open={dialogOpen}
   148‚Üí  onClose={() => setDialogOpen(false)}
   149‚Üí  onSubmit={async (fields) => { await LivingAppsService.createKurseEntry(fields); fetchAll(); }} // dialog closes itself on success
   150‚Üí  defaultValues={editRecord?.fields}         // undefined = create, fields = edit
   151‚Üí  dozentenList={dozenten}                    // list prop = {entityIdentifier}List ‚Äî matches useDashboardData key exactly
   152‚Üí  raeumeList={raeume}                        // dozenten ‚Üí dozentenList, raeume ‚Üí raeumeList (NOT dozentList/raumList)
   153‚Üí  enablePhotoScan={AI_PHOTO_SCAN['Kurse']}   // import AI_PHOTO_SCAN from '@/config/ai-features'
   154‚Üí/>
   155‚Üí```
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí**Applookup `defaultValues` need full record URLs ‚Äî NEVER raw IDs:**
   158‚Üí```tsx
   159‚Üí// ‚ùå WRONG ‚Äî raw ID breaks the Select
   160‚ÜídefaultValues={{ kurs: selectedKursId }}
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üí// ‚úÖ CORRECT
   163‚Üíimport { APP_IDS } from '@/types/app';
   164‚Üíimport { createRecordUrl } from '@/services/livingAppsService';
   165‚ÜídefaultValues={{ kurs: createRecordUrl(APP_IDS.KURSE, selectedKursId) }}
   166‚Üí```
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí**`StatCard`** ‚Äî `icon` must be rendered JSX, NOT a component reference:
   169‚Üí```tsx
   170‚Üí// ‚úÖ CORRECT
   171‚Üí<StatCard title="Kurse" value="42" description="Gesamt" icon={<BookOpen size={18} className="text-muted-foreground" />} />
   172‚Üí// ‚ùå WRONG ‚Äî causes runtime error
   173‚Üí<StatCard icon={BookOpen} />
   174‚Üí```
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí**`ConfirmDialog`** ‚Äî uses `onClose` (not `onCancel`):
   177‚Üí```tsx
   178‚Üí<ConfirmDialog
   179‚Üí  open={!!deleteTarget}
   180‚Üí  title="Eintrag l√∂schen"
   181‚Üí  description="Wirklich l√∂schen?"
   182‚Üí  onConfirm={handleDelete}
   183‚Üí  onClose={() => setDeleteTarget(null)}
   184‚Üí/>
   185‚Üí```
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí## Critical Implementation Rules
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí### Type Imports
   190‚Üí```typescript
   191‚Üí// ‚ùå WRONG
   192‚Üíimport { Workout } from '@/types/app';
   193‚Üí// ‚úÖ CORRECT
   194‚Üíimport type { Workout } from '@/types/app';
   195‚Üí```
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üí### extractRecordId Null Check
   198‚Üí```typescript
   199‚Üíconst id = extractRecordId(record.fields.relation);
   200‚Üíif (!id) return;
   201‚Üí```
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí### Dates Without Seconds
   204‚Üí```typescript
   205‚Üíconst dateForAPI = formData.date + 'T12:00'; // YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM only
   206‚Üí```
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí### Select Never Empty Value
   209‚Üí```typescript
   210‚Üí// ‚ùå <SelectItem value="">None</SelectItem>
   211‚Üí// ‚úÖ <SelectItem value="none">None</SelectItem>
   212‚Üí```
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí---
   215‚Üí
   216‚Üí## Completeness Checklist
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí### Core Component
   219‚Üí- [ ] Interactive component implements the chosen UI paradigm
   220‚Üí- [ ] Users can create, edit, delete directly from the dashboard
   221‚Üí- [ ] Component takes significant screen space (hero element)
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí### Technical
   224‚Üí- [ ] `npm run build` passes
   225‚Üí- [ ] Empty state handled (loading/error are pre-generated)
   226‚Üí- [ ] No hardcoded demo data
   227‚Üí- [ ] Responsive: mobile and desktop layouts
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí---
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí## Living Apps API Reference
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí### Date Formats (STRICT!)
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí| Field Type | Format | Example |
   236‚Üí|------------|--------|---------|
   237‚Üí| `date/date` | `YYYY-MM-DD` | `2025-11-06` |
   238‚Üí| `date/datetimeminute` | `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM` | `2025-11-06T12:00` |
   239‚Üí
   240‚ÜíNO seconds for `datetimeminute`!
   241‚Üí
   242‚Üí### applookup Fields
   243‚Üí
   244‚ÜíStore full URLs: `https://my.living-apps.de/rest/apps/{app_id}/records/{record_id}`
   245‚Üí
   246‚Üí```typescript
   247‚Üíimport { extractRecordId, createRecordUrl } from '@/services/livingAppsService';
   248‚Üíimport { APP_IDS } from '@/types/app';
   249‚Üí
   250‚Üíconst recordId = extractRecordId(record.fields.category);
   251‚Üíif (!recordId) return;
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üíconst data = { category: createRecordUrl(APP_IDS.CATEGORIES, selectedId) };
   254‚Üí```
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí### API Response Format
   257‚Üí
   258‚ÜíReturns **object**, NOT array. Use `Object.entries()` to extract `record_id`.
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üí---
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí## Data Access (pre-generated ‚Äî do NOT rewrite)
   263‚Üí
   264‚ÜíAll data fetching, lookup maps, and enrichment are pre-generated. In DashboardOverview.tsx:
   265‚Üí
   266‚Üí```typescript
   267‚Üí// Already in the skeleton ‚Äî just use the data:
   268‚Üíconst { kurse, anmeldungen, dozentenMap, loading, error, fetchAll } = useDashboardData();
   269‚Üíconst enrichedKurse = enrichKurse(kurse, dozentenMap, raeumeMap);
   270‚Üí```
   271‚Üí
   272‚ÜíFor CRUD after user actions:
   273‚Üí
   274‚Üí```typescript
   275‚Üíconst handleCreate = async (fields) => {
   276‚Üí  await LivingAppsService.createKurseEntry(fields);
   277‚Üí  fetchAll();
   278‚Üí};
   279‚Üí
   280‚Üíconst handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
   281‚Üí  await LivingAppsService.deleteKurseEntry(id);
   282‚Üí  fetchAll();
   283‚Üí};
   284‚Üí```
   285‚Üí
   286‚Üí## Chart Pattern (recharts)
   287‚Üí
   288‚Üí```typescript
   289‚Üíimport { LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí<ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={300}>
   292‚Üí  <LineChart data={data}>
   293‚Üí    <XAxis dataKey="name" stroke="var(--muted-foreground)" />
   294‚Üí    <YAxis stroke="var(--muted-foreground)" />
   295‚Üí    <Tooltip contentStyle={{ backgroundColor: 'var(--background)', border: '1px solid var(--border)' }} />
   296‚Üí    <Line type="monotone" dataKey="value" stroke="var(--primary)" strokeWidth={2} dot={false} />
   297‚Üí  </LineChart>
   298‚Üí</ResponsiveContainer>
   299‚Üí```
   300‚Üí
   301‚Üí## Available Libraries

   303‚Üí- **shadcn/ui** ‚Äî all components in `src/components/ui/`
   304‚Üí- **recharts** ‚Äî LineChart, BarChart, PieChart, AreaChart
   305‚Üí- **lucide-react** ‚Äî icons
   306‚Üí- **date-fns** ‚Äî date formatting with `de` locale
   307‚Üí
   308‚Üí## Formatting (pre-generated ‚Äî just import)

   311‚Üíimport { formatDate, formatCurrency } from '@/lib/formatters';
   312‚Üí

Use this table to guide your choice:

| Data Nature | Best UI Paradigm |
|-------------|-----------------|
| Time-based / scheduled entries | Calendar, week planner, timeline |
| Status-based / workflow stages | Kanban board, progress pipeline |
| Quantitative / goal-tracking | Progress rings, gauges, trend charts |
| Hierarchical / categorized | Grouped sections, nested views |
| Sequential / step-by-step | Stepper, checklist, flow view |
| Relational / many linked items | Master-detail, linked cards |

Then implement immediately. No design_brief.md, no task lists, no planning documents.

**DO NOT touch:** index.css, CRUD pages, dialogs, App.tsx, PageShell.tsx, StatCard.tsx, ConfirmDialog.tsx, ChatWidget.tsx, useDashboardData.ts, enriched.ts, enrich.ts, formatters.ts, ai.ts, chat-context.ts.

`index.css` contains the shared design system (Plus Jakarta Sans, indigo palette, dark sidebar). All semantic tokens (`bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-sidebar`, etc.) are ready to use. Do NOT edit index.css ‚Äî use existing tokens in your components.

## Dashboard = Primary Workspace, NOT Info Page

**The #1 mistake is building the dashboard as a passive info screen** (KPI cards + chart + recent activity). Users want to WORK with their data, not just look at it.

### The Core Interactive Component

Every dashboard needs ONE interactive component ‚Äî the **reason users open the app**. This component:

- Takes up significant screen space (hero, not sidebar widget)
- Supports create, edit, delete directly (click empty slot ‚Üí create dialog, click entry ‚Üí edit)
- Shows data in its most natural form (the paradigm you chose in Step 1)
- Provides immediate visual feedback

The pre-generated CRUD list pages are a fallback. Users should do 90% of their work without leaving the dashboard.

**ALWAYS reuse pre-generated dialogs** ‚Äî When the dashboard needs create/edit forms, import `{Entity}Dialog` from `@/components/dialogs/{Entity}Dialog`. Never build custom dialog forms from scratch ‚Äî the pre-generated ones already have all field types, photo scan, validation, and applookup selects.

### Anti-Slop Checklist (if ANY true, redesign!)

- Dashboard is a passive info page ‚Äî only KPI cards and charts
- No domain-specific UI ‚Äî uses generic list/table for core data
- All KPI cards look identical
- Layout is a boring 2x2 or 3x3 grid
- No clear hero element
- Colors are generic blue/green/red (use the pre-configured palette tokens instead)
- Dashboard could be for ANY app

---

## Design Principles

### Theme

Font (Plus Jakarta Sans) and color palette (indigo accent, warm off-white base, dark sidebar) are pre-configured in `index.css`. Use existing semantic tokens ‚Äî do NOT add custom CSS variables unless the dashboard requires truly app-specific values (e.g. `--calendar-slot-height`).

Create typography hierarchy through weight differences (font-300 vs font-700) and size jumps (text-2xl vs text-sm).

### Layout: Visual Interest Required

Every layout needs variation ‚Äî size, weight, spacing, format, typography. If everything is the same size in identical cards, it's AI slop.

**Mobile:** Vertical flow, thumb-friendly, hero dominates first viewport.
**Desktop:** Use horizontal space, multi-column where appropriate, hover reveals.

## Pre-Generated Component APIs (exact props ‚Äî do NOT Read to check, do NOT guess)

**`{Entity}Dialog`** ‚Äî always this exact interface:
```tsx

  open={dialogOpen}
  onClose={() => setDialogOpen(false)}
```

**Applookup `defaultValues` need full record URLs ‚Äî NEVER raw IDs:**

**`ConfirmDialog`** ‚Äî uses `onClose` (not `onCancel`):

## Living Apps API Reference

### Date Formats (STRICT!)

| Field Type | Format | Example |
|------------|--------|---------|
| `date/date` | `YYYY-MM-DD` | `2025-11-06` |
| `date/datetimeminute` | `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM` | `2025-11-06T12:00` |

NO seconds for `datetimeminute`!

### applookup Fields

Store full URLs: `https://my.living-apps.de/rest/apps/{app_id}/records/{record_id}`

```typescript
import { extractRecordId, createRecordUrl } from '@/services/livingAppsService';
import { APP_IDS } from '@/types/app';

const recordId = extractRecordId(record.fields.category);
if (!recordId) return;

const data = { category: createRecordUrl(APP_IDS.CATEGORIES, selectedId) };
```

### API Response Format

Returns **object**, NOT array. Use `Object.entries()` to extract `record_id`.
